channel.title: Tham Le's Notes
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Tham Le's Notes

item.title: WTF is a Control Plane?
item.description: Why raw WireGuard requires manual configuration for every peer, and how Tailscale, NetBird, and Headscale solve that problem with a coordination layer.

item.title: WTF is a Mesh Network?
item.description: How hub-and-spoke VPNs work, why they fail at scale, and how mesh networking with WireGuard solves the problem. The complete picture of everything in this series.

item.title: WTF is NAT Traversal?
item.description: How WireGuard and Tailscale connect two machines that are both hiding behind NAT, without either one having a public IP.

item.title: WTF is a TUN/TAP Device?
item.description: How userspace programs pretend to be network cards, and why WireGuard, Tailscale, and OpenVPN all depend on it.

item.title: WTF is the WireGuard Protocol?
item.description: A line-by-line look at how WireGuard's Noise handshake, Curve25519 key exchange, and ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption actually work together.

item.title: WTF is JWT?
item.description: JWT explained from structure to cryptography to the attacks that have broken real libraries. What it is, how it works, and where it goes wrong.

item.title: WTF is the OAuth RFC Zoo?
item.description: A map of the OAuth 2.0 RFC ecosystem: which specs matter, what each one fixes, and why the core spec alone is not enough.

item.title: WTF is OAuth 2.0?
item.description: OAuth 2.0 explained from the system design perspective: why it exists, the four actors, and how the flows work.

item.title: WTF is OpenID Connect?
item.description: OpenID Connect explained: the identity layer that sits on top of OAuth 2.0 and actually answers 'who is this user?'

item.title: WTF is SSO?
item.description: Single Sign-On explained: how one login session spans multiple independent apps, the three-session model, and why logout is the hard part.
